constraints: CON-001: Supported languages: Java, Python, TypeScript, JavaScript, C, C++
CON-002: Query response time must be < 1 second (p95)
CON-004: MCP protocol is the primary AI agent interface
CON-005: Repository source is any Git repository (public + private via SSH/token)
CON-006: Indexing must be performed offline in a distributed cluster
CON-013: Index refresh default: weekly, configurable via cron expression
assumptions: ASM-001: Git repositories are accessible from the index cluster (network, credentials)
ASM-002: tree-sitter grammars exist and are stable for all 6 supported languages
ASM-003: CodeSage-large produces consistent 1024-dim vectors across versions
ASM-004: Elasticsearch and Qdrant clusters are provisioned and managed externally
ASM-005: Redis is available for caching; degrade gracefully if unavailable
ASM-006: Primary query traffic comes from AI agents (80%+)

required_configs.name: PostgreSQL URL
required_configs.description: PostgreSQL connection string for repository metadata, API keys, job tracking

required_configs.name: Elasticsearch URL
required_configs.description: Elasticsearch cluster URL for BM25 keyword search

required_configs.name: Qdrant URL
required_configs.description: Qdrant cluster URL for dense vector search

required_configs.name: Redis URL
required_configs.description: Redis instance URL for query caching and rate limiting

required_configs.name: RabbitMQ URL
required_configs.description: RabbitMQ broker URL for Celery task queue

required_configs.name: Repository Clone Path
required_configs.description: Base directory for cloned Git repositories
required_configs.check_hint: Set to /tmp/codecontext/repos or a persistent volume path

required_configs.name: API Secret Key
required_configs.description: Secret key for API key hashing and session signing

required_configs.name: Query API Port
required_configs.description: Port for the FastAPI query service

required_configs.name: MCP Server Port
required_configs.description: Port for the MCP server

required_configs.name: Embedding Model
required_configs.description: Embedding model name for DashScope OpenAI-compatible API

required_configs.name: Embedding API Key
required_configs.description: API key for embedding service (DashScope)

required_configs.name: Embedding Base URL
required_configs.description: Base URL for OpenAI-compatible embedding API

required_configs.name: MiniMax API Key
required_configs.description: API key for MiniMax2.5 Code Plan LLM (evaluation annotation)

required_configs.name: MiniMax Base URL
required_configs.description: Base URL for MiniMax OpenAI-compatible API

required_configs.name: MiniMax Model
required_configs.description: MiniMax model name for evaluation annotation

required_configs.name: Zhipu API Key
required_configs.description: API key for Zhipu CodeGeeX LLM (evaluation annotation)

required_configs.name: Zhipu Base URL
required_configs.description: Base URL for Zhipu OpenAI-compatible API

required_configs.name: Zhipu Model
required_configs.description: Zhipu model name for evaluation annotation

required_configs.name: Eval LLM Provider
required_configs.description: LLM provider for evaluation annotation (minimax or zhipu)

required_configs.name: Reranker Model
required_configs.description: Reranker model name for neural reranking (e.g. qwen3-rerank)

required_configs.name: Reranker API Key
required_configs.description: API key for DashScope reranker service
required_configs.check_hint: Same as EMBEDDING_API_KEY if using DashScope

required_configs.name: Reranker Base URL
required_configs.description: Base URL for reranker API

required_configs.name: Reranker Threshold
required_configs.description: Minimum relevance score threshold for reranker results

features.title: Project Skeleton & CI
features.description: Set up project structure with src/indexing, src/query, src/shared packages, pyproject.toml, pytest config, alembic setup, and basic health check endpoint
verification_steps: Given a fresh clone, when running 'pip install -e .[dev]', then all dependencies install without errors and pytest is available
Given the installed project, when running 'pytest tests/test_skeleton.py', then at least one test passes confirming the project structure exists
Given the project, when importing 'src.shared', 'src.indexing', 'src.query', then all imports succeed without errors
Given alembic config, when running 'alembic check', then it confirms migrations are in sync

features.title: Data Model & Migrations
features.description: Implement SQLAlchemy models for Repository, IndexJob, ApiKey, ApiKeyRepoAccess, QueryLog and create Alembic migration. Implement ES/Qdrant/Redis client wrappers in src/shared/clients/
verification_steps: Given the data models, when running Alembic migration against a test database, then tables REPOSITORY, INDEX_JOB, API_KEY, API_KEY_REPO_ACCESS, QUERY_LOG are created with correct columns and constraints
Given the Repository model, when creating a record with name='test-repo', url='https://github.com/test/repo', then it persists with a generated UUID id, status='pending', and created_at timestamp
Given the storage clients module, when importing ElasticsearchClient, QdrantClient, RedisClient, then all classes are importable and have connection/health-check methods

features.title: Repository Registration
features.description: Implement RepoManager service with register_repo() that validates Git URL, accepts optional branch parameter, checks for duplicates, creates Repository record with indexed_branch, and queues initial indexing job
verification_steps: Given a valid Git URL 'https://github.com/pallets/flask', when calling register_repo(url), then a Repository record is created with status='pending' and the repository ID is returned
Given a valid Git URL with branch='develop', when calling register_repo(url, branch='develop'), then a Repository record is created with indexed_branch='develop'
Given a valid Git URL with no branch parameter, when calling register_repo(url), then a Repository record is created with indexed_branch=None (default branch detected at clone time)
Given an invalid URL 'not-a-url', when calling register_repo(url), then a ValidationError is raised within 2 seconds without creating a record
Given an already-registered URL, when calling register_repo(url) again, then a ConflictError is raised indicating the repository already exists

features.title: Git Clone & Update
features.description: Implement GitCloner with clone_or_update(branch) that performs git clone (with --branch if specified) for new repos or git fetch+reset to origin/{branch} for existing repos, detects default_branch, supports list_remote_branches(), with error handling for network/auth/disk failures
verification_steps: Given a newly registered repository with no branch specified, when clone_or_update() runs, then the repo is cloned to REPO_CLONE_PATH/{repo_id}, default_branch is detected via symbolic-ref, and indexed_branch is set to default_branch
Given a newly registered repository with branch='develop', when clone_or_update(branch='develop') runs, then the repo is cloned with --branch develop and indexed_branch is set to 'develop'
Given a previously cloned repository with indexed_branch='main', when clone_or_update(branch='main') runs again, then it performs git fetch and resets to origin/main without re-cloning
Given a clone operation that fails (unreachable URL), when clone_or_update() runs, then it raises a CloneError with the error message and cleans up partial files
Given a cloned repository, when list_remote_branches() is called, then it returns a sorted list of remote branch names with origin/ prefix stripped

features.title: Content Extraction
features.description: Implement ContentExtractor that classifies files by type (source, doc, example, rule) by extension/path patterns, skips binary/oversized files, and reads text content
verification_steps: Given a cloned repo with .py, .java, .md, and .png files, when extract_content() runs, then source files are classified as 'code', .md files as 'doc', and .png files are skipped
Given a file larger than 1MB, when extraction runs, then the file is skipped with a logged warning
Given files in examples/ directory, when extraction runs, then they are classified as 'example' content type
Given CLAUDE.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, .cursor/rules files, when extraction runs, then they are classified as 'rule' content type

features.title: Code Chunking
features.description: Implement Chunker with tree-sitter AST parsing for 6 languages, producing L1 (file), L2 (class), L3 (function) chunks with symbol, signature, doc_comment extraction. Includes DocChunker for markdown heading-based splitting and RuleExtractor for rule files.
verification_steps: Given a Python file with 1 class containing 3 methods, when chunk_file() runs, then it produces 1 L1 file chunk, 1 L2 class chunk, and 3 L3 function chunks with correct symbol names and signatures
Given a Java file with 2 classes, when chunk_file() runs, then it produces L1, L2, and L3 chunks with parent_class set correctly for methods
Given a README.md with H1, H2, H3 headings, when chunk_markdown() runs, then it produces doc chunks split at H2 level with breadcrumb trails (e.g., 'Getting Started > Installation')
Given a file in an unsupported language, when chunk_file() runs, then it falls back to L1 file-level chunking only

features.title: Embedding Generation
features.description: Implement EmbeddingEncoder using CodeSage-large model via sentence-transformers, with encode_documents() for batch indexing and encode_query() with instruction prefix for query-time embedding. Write embeddings to Qdrant.
verification_steps: Given a list of 10 code chunks, when encode_documents() runs in batch, then it produces 10 vectors of 1024 dimensions each as float32 arrays
Given a query string 'how to configure timeout', when encode_query() runs, then it prepends 'Represent this code search query: ' and returns a single 1024-dim vector
Given 100 chunks with embeddings, when IndexWriter.write_code_chunks() runs, then all 100 vectors are stored in the Qdrant 'code_embeddings' collection with correct payload metadata

features.title: Keyword Retrieval (BM25)
features.description: Implement Retriever.bm25_search() using Elasticsearch with the code_analyzer for code_chunks index and doc_analyzer for doc_chunks index, returning top-200 scored candidates
verification_steps: Given indexed code chunks containing 'getUserName', when bm25_search('getUserName') runs against code_chunks, then results include chunks containing that symbol, ranked by BM25 score
Given the code_analyzer with synonym filter, when searching 'auth', then results also include chunks containing 'authentication' and 'authorization'
Given a query with no matching terms, when bm25_search() runs, then it returns an empty list without error
Given Elasticsearch is unreachable, when bm25_search() runs, then it raises a retrieval error (caller handles degradation)
Given a branch parameter, BM25 search adds term filter on branch field

features.title: Semantic Retrieval (Vector)
features.description: Implement Retriever.vector_search() using Qdrant HNSW search with cosine similarity, encoding the query via EmbeddingEncoder.encode_query() and returning top-200 candidates
verification_steps: Given indexed embeddings for code about HTTP timeouts, when vector_search('how to configure spring http client timeout') runs, then results include semantically related chunks even without exact term matches
Given a query vector, when vector_search() runs, then it returns up to 200 candidates with cosine similarity scores from the Qdrant collection
Given Qdrant is unreachable, when vector_search() runs, then it raises a retrieval error (caller handles degradation)
Given a branch parameter, vector search adds payload filter on branch field

features.title: Rank Fusion (RRF)
features.description: Implement RankFusion.fuse() using Reciprocal Rank Fusion with k=60, merging up to 5 ranked lists (4 primary + optional symbol boost) into a single top-50 candidate list
verification_steps: Given 200 BM25 candidates and 200 vector candidates with 50 overlapping chunks, when fuse() runs with k=60, then overlapping chunks receive boosted RRF scores from both rankings and the output contains at most 50 candidates
Given one empty result list and one non-empty list, when fuse() runs, then it returns results from the non-empty list only
Given 4-way input (BM25 code, vector code, BM25 doc, vector doc), when fuse() runs, then all 4 lists are merged by RRF and execution completes within 10ms

features.title: Neural Reranking
features.description: Implement Reranker using bge-reranker-v2-m3 cross-encoder via sentence-transformers, scoring query-document pairs and selecting top-K results with fallback to RRF order on model failure
verification_steps: Given 50 fused candidates and a query, when rerank() runs, then it returns top-6 candidates re-scored by the cross-encoder with relevance scores
Given fewer than 3 candidates, when rerank() runs, then it returns all available candidates without error
Given a reranker model failure (OOM, load error), when rerank() runs, then it falls back to the fusion-ranked order and logs a degradation warning

features.title: Context Response Builder
features.description: Implement ResponseBuilder that assembles the dual-list response format: codeResults[] + docResults[] + rules{}, splitting reranked results by content_type, truncating content to 2000 chars
verification_steps: Given 6 reranked results (3 code, 2 doc, 1 example), when build() runs, then the response contains codeResults with 3 items and docResults with 3 items (doc + example), each with content, repo, path, symbol, type, language, score fields
Given a chunk with content exceeding 2000 characters, when build() runs, then the content is truncated to 2000 chars with a trailing '...' indicator
Given a repository with rule chunks, when build() runs with include_rules=true, then the response includes a rules section with the full rule content

features.title: Natural Language Query Handler
features.description: Implement QueryHandler.handle_nl_query() that runs the full hybrid pipeline: 4-way parallel retrieval, unified RRF fusion, single rerank, with query expansion for embedded code identifiers
verification_steps: Given the NL query 'how to use grpc java interceptor', when handle_nl_query() runs, then it executes 4-way parallel retrieval (BM25 code + vector code + BM25 doc + vector doc), fuses with RRF, reranks, and returns structured context results
Given an empty query string, when handle_nl_query() is called, then it raises a ValidationError with a descriptive message
Given the NL query 'how does AuthService validate tokens', when query expansion runs, then it extracts 'AuthService' and fires a parallel symbol.raw term query merged into RRF with weight 0.3
Given a pipeline that exceeds 1-second timeout, when handle_nl_query() runs, then it returns partial results with degraded=true flag
Given repo in owner/repo@branch format, branch is parsed and forwarded to retriever

features.title: Symbol Query Handler
features.description: Implement QueryHandler.handle_symbol_query() with auto-detection heuristic (dots, camelCase, snake_case, ::, #), ES term exact match → fuzzy fallback → NL pipeline fallback
verification_steps: Given the query 'UserService.getById', when detect_query_type() runs, then it classifies as 'symbol' due to dot notation
Given the symbol query 'std::vector', when handle_symbol_query() runs, then it first tries ES term query on symbol.raw, and returns matching class definitions and methods
Given a symbol query for a non-existent symbol, when ES term and fuzzy queries return 0 results, then it falls back to the full NL pipeline using the query as-is
Given a symbol query exceeding 200 characters, when submitted, then it raises a ValidationError
Given repo in owner/repo@branch format, branch is parsed and forwarded to retriever

features.title: Repository-Scoped Query
features.description: Implement repository filtering in Retriever that restricts BM25 and vector searches to chunks from a specified repo_id, applied as ES filter clause and Qdrant payload filter
verification_steps: Given query 'timeout' with repo_filter='spring-framework', when retrieval runs, then all returned chunks have repo_id matching 'spring-framework'
Given a repo_filter for a non-existent repository, when retrieval runs, then it returns an empty result set with 200 status
Given a query without repo_filter, when retrieval runs, then it searches across all indexed repositories

features.title: API Key Authentication
features.description: Implement AuthMiddleware for FastAPI with API key validation against PostgreSQL, Redis-cached lookups, rate limiting (10 failed attempts/IP/minute → 429), role-based access (read/admin), and API key CRUD (create/revoke/rotate/list)
verification_steps: Given a valid API key in X-API-Key header, when a request is made, then the middleware allows the request and attaches the key's role to the request state
Given an invalid or missing API key, when a request is made, then the middleware returns 401 Unauthorized without executing the handler
Given more than 10 failed auth attempts from the same IP within 1 minute, when another attempt is made, then the middleware returns 429 Too Many Requests
Given APIKeyManager.create_key(name='test', role='read'), when called, then it generates a key, stores the SHA-256 hash in PostgreSQL, and returns the plaintext key once

features.title: REST API Endpoints
features.description: Implement FastAPI router with endpoints: POST /api/v1/query, GET /api/v1/repos, POST /api/v1/repos, POST /api/v1/repos/{repo_id}/reindex, GET /api/v1/health, and API key management endpoints
verification_steps: Given a POST to /api/v1/query with valid query body and API key, when processed, then it returns 200 with structured context results (codeResults + docResults + rules)
Given a GET to /api/v1/repos with valid API key, when processed, then it returns the list of registered repositories with their indexing status
Given a GET to /api/v1/health without authentication, when processed, then it returns service health status with ES/Qdrant/Redis connectivity
Given a malformed JSON body on POST /api/v1/query, when submitted, then it returns 400 with a validation error message
POST /api/v1/query without repo_id returns 422 (required field)
POST /api/v1/query with repo_id='owner/repo@branch' parses branch and filters results

features.title: MCP Server
features.description: Implement standalone MCP server exposing 3 tools: search_code_context (with max_tokens param), list_repositories, get_chunk. Uses the same query handler as REST API.
verification_steps: resolve_repository(query, libraryName) returns indexed repos with branches
search_code_context(query, repo) with repo required returns scoped results
search_code_context(query, repo='owner/repo@branch') parses branch and filters
search_code_context without repo raises TypeError
get_chunk(chunk_id) returns full chunk content
MCP error response on invalid params (no crash)
MCP error response on internal retrieval failure (no crash)

features.title: Web UI Search Page
features.description: Implement Jinja2 SSR web interface with search input, repository filter dropdown, language filter checkboxes, branch selector for registration, syntax-highlighted results with htmx partial updates. Developer Dark theme per UCD.
verification_steps: [devtools] Navigate / → EXPECT: search input field, repository filter dropdown, language filter checkboxes rendered with Developer Dark theme (bg #1a1b26, text #c0caf5); fill query 'timeout' → click Search → EXPECT: results displayed with syntax-highlighted code snippets, repo name, file path, symbol name, relevance score; REJECT: console errors, broken styles
[devtools] Navigate / → submit empty query → EXPECT: validation message 'Please enter a search query'; REJECT: server error, blank results
Given no results for a query, when displayed, then the UI shows a 'No results found' message with the Developer Dark empty state styling

[devtools] Navigate to repository registration form → enter a repo URL → EXPECT: branch selector dropdown populated with available branches (fetched via Branch Listing API), defaulting to the repo's default branch; select a branch → submit → EXPECT: repository registered with the selected branch; REJECT: empty branch dropdown, console errors, missing default selection
Repository dropdown is required (no 'all repos' option)
Dropdown only shows status=indexed repositories

features.title: Language Filter
features.description: Implement LanguageFilter that adds ES/Qdrant filter clauses restricting results to specified programming languages, applied before retrieval
verification_steps: Given query 'timeout' with language_filter=['java'], when retrieval runs, then all returned code chunks have language='java'
Given multiple language filters ['java', 'python'], when retrieval runs, then returned chunks are in either Java or Python
Given an unrecognized language value 'rust', when submitted, then a ValidationError is raised listing supported languages
Given an empty language filter list, when retrieval runs, then no language filtering is applied

features.title: Scheduled Index Refresh
features.description: Implement Celery Beat periodic task that triggers re-indexing for all registered repositories on a configurable cron schedule (default: weekly Sunday 02:00 UTC)
verification_steps: Given the default cron configuration, when the scheduler fires, then it queues re-indexing jobs for all registered repositories with status='active'
Given a scheduled job that fails, when the failure is detected, then the system retries once after 1 hour; if retry also fails, it logs an error and skips until next window
Given a re-index already in progress for a repository, when the schedule fires, then it skips the duplicate and logs an informational message

features.title: Manual Reindex Trigger
features.description: Implement POST /api/v1/repos/{repo_id}/reindex endpoint that queues an immediate re-indexing job for a specific repository, requiring admin role
verification_steps: Given a POST to /api/v1/repos/{repo_id}/reindex with valid admin API key, when processed, then an indexing job is queued and the response contains job_id with status='queued'
Given a reindex request for a non-existent repository, when processed, then it returns 404
Given a reindex request with a read-only API key, when processed, then it returns 403 Forbidden

features.title: Metrics Endpoint
features.description: Implement /metrics endpoint using prometheus-client exposing query_latency_seconds, retrieval_latency_seconds, rerank_latency_seconds, index_size_chunks, queries_per_second, cache_hit_ratio
verification_steps: Given a GET to /metrics without authentication, when processed, then the response contains Prometheus text format with all required metric names: query_latency_seconds, retrieval_latency_seconds, rerank_latency_seconds, index_size_chunks, cache_hit_ratio
Given several queries have been processed, when /metrics is called, then the histogram buckets for query_latency_seconds contain observed values

features.title: Query Logging
features.description: Implement structured JSON logging for query service: each query logs query, query_type, api_key_id, result_count, retrieval_ms, rerank_ms, total_ms, timestamp to stdout. Non-fatal.
verification_steps: Given a completed query, when logging executes, then a structured JSON log entry is written to stdout containing: query, query_type, result_count, retrieval_ms, rerank_ms, total_ms, timestamp
Given a logging I/O failure, when it occurs during query processing, then the query response is not blocked or delayed

features.title: Query Cache
features.description: Implement Cache with Redis backend and in-memory L1 cache. Cache key = hash(query + repo_filter + language_filter). TTL=300s. Cache invalidation on reindex.
verification_steps: Given a query that has been cached, when the same query is submitted again within TTL, then the cached result is returned without executing the retrieval pipeline
Given a cached query result, when a repository reindex completes, then the cache entries for that repository are invalidated
Given Redis is unavailable, when cache operations are attempted, then the system degrades gracefully to no-cache mode without errors

features.title: NFR-001: Query Latency p95 < 1s
features.description: Verify that p95 query latency is under 1000ms end-to-end under load (100 concurrent users)
verification_steps: Given a Locust load test with 100 concurrent users sending diverse NL and symbol queries, when run for 5 minutes, then p95 end-to-end latency is < 1000ms

features.title: NFR-002: Query Throughput >= 1000 QPS
features.description: Verify sustained throughput of >= 1000 QPS with acceptable error rate
verification_steps: Given a Locust load test ramping to 1000+ QPS, when sustained for 5 minutes, then the system maintains >= 1000 QPS with < 1% error rate

features.title: NFR-003: Repository Capacity
features.description: Verify the system can index and serve queries across 100-1000 repositories
verification_steps: Given 100+ registered repositories with indexed content, when querying across all repos, then results are returned within the latency budget

features.title: NFR-004: Single Repository Size
features.description: Verify the system handles repositories up to 1GB in size
verification_steps: Given a repository approaching 1GB, when indexing runs, then all files are processed and chunks are indexed without OOM or timeout

features.title: NFR-005: Service Availability 99.9%
features.description: Verify query service achieves 99.9% uptime with health monitoring
verification_steps: Given the query service running with health check endpoint, when monitored over a test window, then uptime exceeds 99.9% with automatic recovery from transient failures

features.title: NFR-006: Linear Scalability >= 70%
features.description: Verify adding query nodes yields >= 70% of theoretical throughput increase
verification_steps: Given N query nodes achieving X QPS, when deploying N+1 nodes, then throughput increases by >= 0.7 * (X/N)

features.title: NFR-007: Single-Node Failure Tolerance
features.description: Verify query service continues operating when any single node fails
verification_steps: Given a multi-node query cluster under load, when one node is killed, then remaining nodes continue serving requests without failures

features.title: Branch Listing API
features.description: Implement GET /api/v1/repos/{id}/branches endpoint that returns list of remote branches for a registered repository, using GitCloner.list_remote_branches(). Returns 404 for unknown repos, 409 if not yet cloned.
verification_steps: Given a registered and cloned repository, when GET /api/v1/repos/{id}/branches is called, then it returns 200 with {branches: ['main', 'develop', ...], default_branch: 'main'} sorted alphabetically
Given a repository ID that does not exist, when the endpoint is called, then it returns 404 Not Found
Given a repository that has not been cloned yet, when the endpoint is called, then it returns 409 Conflict

features.title: Python: decorated_definition Unwrapping
features.description: Unwrap decorated_definition AST nodes in Python so that @property, @staticmethod, @classmethod, @dataclass, @app.route and other decorator-wrapped functions produce L3 chunks and decorator-wrapped classes produce L2 chunks. Decorator text preserved in chunk content.
verification_steps: Given a Python file with @property-decorated getter and @property.setter, when chunking completes, then each produces an L3 chunk with correct symbol name and parent_class
Given a Python file with @staticmethod and @classmethod decorated functions inside a class, when chunking completes, then each produces an L3 chunk with parent_class set to the class name
Given a Python file with @dataclass decorated class containing fields, when chunking completes, then the class produces an L2 chunk with symbol equal to the class name
Given a Python file with a top-level @app.route('/') decorated function, when chunking completes, then the function produces an L3 chunk with parent_class empty

features.title: Java: enum + record + static initializer
features.description: Add enum_declaration and record_declaration to Java class_nodes so enums (with methods) and records produce L2 chunks. Add static_initializer to function_nodes so static {} blocks produce L3 chunks.
verification_steps: Given a Java file with an enum declaration containing 2 enum constants and 1 method, when chunking completes, then the enum produces an L2 chunk and the method produces an L3 chunk with parent_class equal to the enum name
Given a Java file with a record declaration (Java 16+), when chunking completes, then the record produces an L2 chunk with correct symbol name
Given a Java file with a static initializer block inside a class, when chunking completes, then the static block produces an L3 chunk with parent_class set to the class name

features.title: JavaScript: prototype-assigned functions + require() imports
features.description: Detect expression_statement > assignment_expression where right side is function_expression or arrow_function, extracting property name as symbol for L3 chunk. Detect require() calls in variable_declaration and extract module path as import.
verification_steps: Given a JavaScript file with res.status = function(code){...}, when chunking completes, then an L3 chunk is produced with symbol='status'
Given a JavaScript file with obj.handler = (req, res) => {...}, when chunking completes, then an L3 chunk is produced with symbol='handler'
Given a JavaScript file with var express = require('express'), when chunking completes, then the L1 file chunk's imports list includes 'express'
Given a JavaScript file with multiple require() calls, when chunking completes, then all required module paths appear in imports

features.title: TypeScript: enum + namespace + decorator unwrapping
features.description: Add enum_declaration to TS class_nodes. Unwrap internal_module/module (namespace) to recurse into body for class/function detection. Unwrap decorator-wrapped classes/functions (same strategy as Python decorated_definition).
verification_steps: Given a TypeScript file with an enum declaration containing 3 members, when chunking completes, then the enum produces an L2 chunk
Given a TypeScript file with namespace Foo { class Bar { method() {} } }, when chunking completes, then Bar produces an L2 chunk and method produces an L3 chunk
Given a TypeScript file with nested namespaces (namespace A { namespace B { class C {} } }), when chunking completes, then class C produces an L2 chunk
Given a TypeScript file with @Component-decorated class, when chunking completes, then the class produces an L2 chunk with correct symbol name

features.title: C: typedef struct + function prototypes + enum
features.description: Detect type_definition containing struct_specifier as L2 chunk with typedef name as symbol. Detect declaration nodes with function_declarator (no body) as L3 prototype chunks. Add enum_specifier to C class_nodes.
verification_steps: Given a C file with typedef struct { int x; } Point;, when chunking completes, then an L2 chunk is produced with symbol='Point'
Given a C header file with 5 function prototype declarations (no body, semicolon-terminated), when chunking completes, then 5 L3 chunks are produced with content equal to each declaration line
Given a C file with enum Color { RED, GREEN, BLUE };, when chunking completes, then an L2 chunk is produced with symbol='Color'
Given a C header with both typedef structs and function prototypes inside #ifndef guards, when chunking completes, then all are correctly detected (not blocked by preproc_ifdef)

features.title: C++: namespace + template unwrapping
features.description: Recursively unwrap namespace_definition nodes (including nested and C++17 inline namespaces) to find class/function nodes within. Single-level unwrap template_declaration to detect inner class_specifier or function_definition.
verification_steps: Given a C++ file with namespace foo { class Bar { void method() {} } }, when chunking completes, then Bar produces an L2 chunk and method produces an L3 chunk
Given a C++ file with nested namespaces (namespace a { namespace b { void func() {} } }), when chunking completes, then func produces an L3 chunk
Given a C++ file with template<typename T> class Container { void push(T val) {} }, when chunking completes, then Container produces an L2 chunk and push produces an L3 chunk
Given a C++ file with template<typename T> T max_val(T a, T b) {}, when chunking completes, then max_val produces an L3 chunk

features.title: Evaluation Corpus Management
features.description: Build evaluation corpus by cloning representative open-source repos (2 per supported language, 12 total from eval/repos.json), running the full indexing pipeline (ContentExtractor → Chunker → EmbeddingEncoder → IndexWriter) into eval_ prefixed ES/Qdrant namespaces. Idempotent — skips already-indexed repos.
verification_steps: Given eval/repos.json with 12 repos (2 per language), when EvalCorpusBuilder.build() runs, then all accessible repos are cloned and indexed with chunks in eval_code_chunks (ES) and eval_code_embeddings (Qdrant)
Given a previously built corpus, when EvalCorpusBuilder.build() runs again, then it skips already-indexed repos without re-indexing (idempotent)
Given an inaccessible repo URL in the list, when EvalCorpusBuilder.build() runs, then it logs an error and continues with remaining repos

features.title: LLM Query Generation & Relevance Annotation
features.description: Use OpenAI-compatible LLM API with multi-provider support (MiniMax, Zhipu) via EVAL_LLM_PROVIDER env var to generate 50-100 NL queries per evaluation repo across 4 categories, retrieve top-20 chunks per query, and annotate relevance (0-3 scale) using dual annotation with Cohen Kappa consistency checking. Store as golden dataset in eval/golden/{slug}.json.
verification_steps: Given an indexed evaluation repo, when LLMAnnotator.generate_queries() runs, then 50-100 NL queries are produced covering API usage, bug diagnosis, configuration, and architecture understanding categories
Given a (query, chunk) pair, when dual annotation runs, then two independent scores (0-3) are produced; if they disagree by >1, a third annotation resolves via majority vote
Given all annotations complete, when Cohen Kappa is computed, then the inter-annotator agreement score is recorded in the golden dataset metadata
Given the golden dataset is saved, then eval/golden/{slug}.json contains query text, repo_id, language, category, and per-chunk annotations with scores
Given EVAL_LLM_PROVIDER='zhipu' and valid Zhipu credentials, when LLMAnnotator runs, then it uses the Zhipu API endpoint and produces annotations in the same format as MiniMax

features.title: Retrieval Quality Evaluation & Reporting
features.description: Evaluate retrieval pipeline stages (vector-only, BM25-only, RRF, reranked) against the golden dataset using standard IR metrics (MRR@10, NDCG@10, Recall@200, Precision@3). Generate Markdown report with per-language and per-stage breakdowns. Unimplemented stages marked N/A. Delta comparison with previous report.
verification_steps: Given a golden dataset and vector_code_search available, when EvalRunner.evaluate_stage("vector") runs, then MRR@10, NDCG@10, Recall@200, and Precision@3 are computed correctly
Given a retrieval stage not yet implemented, when EvalRunner.evaluate_stage() runs for that stage, then it returns N/A metrics without error
Given evaluation results, when ReportGenerator.generate() runs, then a Markdown report is saved to eval/reports/YYYY-MM-DD-eval-report.md with per-language and per-stage breakdowns
Given a previous report exists, when a new evaluation runs, then the report includes a delta section comparing metrics

features.title: query-api Docker Image
features.description: Dockerfile for the codecontext-api image. Builds a Python 3.11-slim image with production deps only, runs src/query/main.py (new production entrypoint), exposes port 8000, includes HEALTHCHECK targeting /api/v1/health. Also creates src/query/main.py which wires all production services from env vars and starts uvicorn.
verification_steps: Given docker/Dockerfile.api exists, when docker build -f docker/Dockerfile.api -t codecontext-api . runs, then the build exits 0
Given the built image is run with required env vars, when the container starts, then GET /api/v1/health returns 200 within 30s
Given the image is inspected with docker inspect, then a HEALTHCHECK instruction targeting port 8000 is present
Given the image is built, then it contains only production dependencies (no pytest or dev extras)
Given the image is built, then it runs as a non-root user (UID != 0)

features.title: mcp-server Docker Image
features.description: Dockerfile for the codecontext-mcp image. Builds a Python 3.11-slim image with production deps + git + ca-certificates, runs python -m src.query.mcp_server (streamable-http MCP protocol on port 3000, path /mcp), EXPOSE 3000, HEALTHCHECK probes the TCP listener.
verification_steps: Given docker/Dockerfile.mcp exists, when docker build -f docker/Dockerfile.mcp -t codecontext-mcp . runs, then the build exits 0
Given the built image is run detached without -i, when the container starts, then it stays running and binds port 3000
Given the image is inspected, then a HEALTHCHECK that probes the streamable-http listener on port 3000 is present
Given the image is built, then it contains production dependencies + git, runs as a non-root user, and EXPOSE 3000
Given the running container receives a POST /mcp initialize JSON-RPC, then it returns serverInfo.name=code-context-retrieval

features.title: index-worker Docker Image
features.description: Dockerfile for the codecontext-worker image. Builds a Python 3.11-slim image with production deps only, runs celery -A src.indexing.celery_app worker, includes HEALTHCHECK using celery inspect ping.
verification_steps: Given docker/Dockerfile.worker exists, when docker build -f docker/Dockerfile.worker -t codecontext-worker . runs, then the build exits 0
Given the built image is run with CELERY_BROKER_URL set, when the container starts, then celery -A src.indexing.celery_app worker is the active process
Given the image is inspected, then a HEALTHCHECK using celery inspect ping is present
Given the image is built, then it contains only production dependencies and runs as a non-root user

features.name: Repository Resolution MCP Tool
verification_steps: resolve_repository(query='JSON parse', libraryName='gson') returns indexed repos with name match
Each result includes id, name, url, indexed_branch, default_branch, available_branches, last_indexed_at
libraryName matching no indexed repo returns empty list
Missing query or libraryName parameter raises TypeError
Results sorted by name match quality (exact > prefix > substring)
features.title: Repository Resolution MCP Tool
features.description: resolve_repository MCP tool with query+libraryName required params, returns indexed repos with branches (Context7-aligned)

features.name: Web UI Index Management Page
features.title: Web UI Index Management Page
verification_steps: [devtools] Navigate to /admin/indexes → page renders with table of all registered repos (name, status, branch, last_indexed_at)
[devtools] Click Stats on a repo → inline display shows ES doc counts (code_chunks, doc_chunks, rule_chunks) and Qdrant counts (code_embeddings, doc_embeddings)
Click Reindex on a repo → Celery task dispatched, success message with job ID displayed
[devtools] Click Reindex All → confirmation prompt shown; upon confirm, tasks dispatched for all indexed repos with summary message
[devtools] Click Delete Index on a repo → confirmation prompt shown; upon confirm, all index data deleted, success message displayed
All actions use HTMX partial updates without full page reload
Index management routes are NOT accessible via MCP tools
features.description: Admin page at /admin/indexes for managing repository indexes — list, stats, reindex, reindex-all, delete with confirmation prompts. Web UI only.

features.name: Fix delete_repo_index branch filter
features.title: Fix delete_repo_index branch filter on doc/rule indices
verification_steps: Given a repo with indexed doc_chunks and rule_chunks, when delete_repo_index is called, then doc_chunks and rule_chunks are deleted using repo_id-only filter (no branch field)
Given a repo with indexed code_chunks and code_embeddings, when delete_repo_index is called, then code_chunks and code_embeddings are deleted using repo_id+branch filter
Given a repo with doc_embeddings, when delete_repo_index is called, then doc_embeddings are deleted using repo_id-only filter
features.description: Fix IndexWriter.delete_repo_index() to use repo_id-only filter for doc/rule indices that have no branch field

features.name: Fix reindex API Celery dispatch
features.title: Fix reindex API endpoint not dispatching Celery task
verification_steps: Given POST /api/v1/repos/{repo_id}/reindex with valid admin key, when processed, then reindex_repo_task.delay() is called with the repo ID
Given the Celery task dispatch fails, when the endpoint is called, then the IndexJob record is still created and the endpoint returns successfully
features.description: Fix reindex API endpoint to dispatch Celery task after creating IndexJob record

features.name: Add psycopg2-binary dependency
features.title: Add psycopg2-binary for Celery worker sync DB access
verification_steps: Given psycopg2-binary is listed in pyproject.toml dev dependencies, when pip install -e '.[dev]' runs, then psycopg2 is importable
Given the Celery worker starts, when _get_sync_session() is called, then a sync PostgreSQL session is created without ImportError
features.description: Add psycopg2-binary>=2.9 to pyproject.toml dev dependencies for Celery worker sync DB sessions

waves.description: Support branch selection for repository clone & indexing
Strengthen AST parsing accuracy for 6 supported languages — decorator/namespace/template/enum/prototype/require/typedef patterns
Retrieval Quality Evaluation Pipeline — corpus management, LLM annotation, IR metrics & reporting
Modular Docker Images — query-api, mcp-server, index-worker (NFR-012 implementation, release blocker)
Context7-aligned MCP interface: resolve_repository + repo required + @branch
Web UI Index Management + index pipeline bugfixes (delete_repo_index branch filter, reindex API Celery dispatch, psycopg2-binary)
